common.error_boundary_hint: Try reloading the page. If the problem continues, contact support.

trust.title: Trust, security & support
trust.intro: How to reach us for security disclosures and integration support. Operational response times are targets unless contracted.
trust.security_body: Report vulnerabilities and coordinate on webhook signing or key compromise.
trust.support_body: For integration questions, dashboard access, and billing semantics.
trust.your_resp_keys: Store API keys and integration webhook secrets securely; rotate credentials if exposed.
trust.your_resp_webhook: Use HTTPS receivers and validate X-Webhook-Signature.
trust.email_not_configured: Configure support email for this deployment.

integrations.title: API keys & webhooks
integrations.lead: Point Armith at your HTTPS endpoint so you receive signed payloads on the specific profile transitions below—not on every API call. Signing secrets are stored server-side and never shown again after save.
integrations.lead_compact: Send signed JSON to your HTTPS endpoint when verifications change. Configure URL, secret, events, and optional payload fields in the card below.
integrations.when_intro: Outbound webhooks run after MongoDB saves the profile. We send at most one POST per qualifying transition. Intermediate states such as waiting for selfie do not emit a terminal webhook.
integrations.event_manual_review_queued_body: When an admin queues manual review so the profile becomes UNDER_REVIEW (from PENDING) via the dashboard / admin API.
integrations.event_manual_review_resolved_body: When an admin resolves manual review with APPROVED or REJECTED and that decision is persisted.
integrations.event_completed_body: When the profile is persisted as APPROVED—for example after selfie approval with your rules, ID-only flows that terminate as approved, or an admin approving from the manual-review queue.
integrations.event_failed_body: When the profile is persisted as REJECTED or FAILED—for example threshold failures, selfie mismatch, admin rejection from manual review, or other terminal failure paths.
integrations.when_not_body: While the session is still non-terminal (e.g. PENDING waiting for the next step), we do not send verification.completed or verification.failed. Only the explicit manual-review queue action emits manual_review_queued.
integrations.replay_note: Admins can replay the latest terminal payload for profiles already APPROVED, REJECTED, or FAILED from the dashboard (resend only; does not change state). Replay ignores your event checkboxes so you can recover a missed POST.
integrations.api_keys_tab_desc: Restrict callers by IP for the whole account (all API keys). On Growth and Enterprise you can add stricter per-key rules.
integrations.api_keys_desc: Create rotated keys and restrict callers by IP—all on the API keys tab above.
integrations.account_ip_desc: When non-empty, every request using your API keys must come from these IPv4/IPv6 addresses or CIDR ranges. This applies to all keys. Leave empty to allow any IP.
integrations.account_ip_list_hint: Add one rule per row—an IP address or CIDR block. Delete a row with Remove; click Save account allowlist to apply changes.
integrations.account_ip_empty: No rules yet. Allowing all IPs. Add rows below if you want to restrict callers.
integrations.per_key_ip_upsell: Per-key IP allowlists (tighter rules per API key) are included on Growth and Enterprise. On Free, use the account allowlist above for all keys.

home.hero_subtitle: Armith uses advanced Vision Language Models to verify ID documents and selfies in seconds. Secure, accurate, and easy to integrate.
home.features_subtitle: A better way to verify users
home.features_desc: Everything you need to build a secure and compliant onboarding flow.
home.feature_fast_desc: Get verification results in seconds using our optimized AI pipeline.
home.feature_global_desc: Support for ID documents from over 190 countries.
home.feature_secure_desc: Your data is encrypted at rest and in transit. We are SOC2 compliant.
home.feature_easy_desc: Integrate with our simple REST API in minutes, not days.
home.cta_desc: Start verifying users for free. No credit card required.

pricing.title: Simple, transparent pricing
pricing.subtitle: Choose the plan that fits your needs. No hidden fees.
pricing.starter_desc: Perfect for testing and small projects.
pricing.growth_up_to_150: $0.15 each up to 150 billable verifications / mo
pricing.growth_after_150: $0.20 each after 150 in the same month
pricing.billable_unit: A billable verification is counted when a subject Profile reaches a terminal status (Approved, Rejected, or Failed) according to your tenant verification steps—not on every intermediate attempt.
pricing.not_billed: Typically not billed: idempotent retries that replay the same outcome within TTL, failures before inference, and webhook delivery attempts (infra is absorbed unless priced separately—see billing docs).
pricing.retention_note: Data handling and deletion are summarized on

upload_id.title: Identity Verification
upload_id.subtitle: Please upload your government-issued ID document.
upload_id.error_file_size: File size must be less than 10MB
upload_id.error_missing_fields: Please select a country and upload the front of your ID
upload_id.demo_mode_notice: Demo mode is active. This flow follows your current plan limits and guardrails.
upload_id.plan_limit_reached: Your monthly verification limit has been reached.

upload_selfie.title: Selfie Verification
upload_selfie.subtitle: Take a selfie to verify your identity.
upload_selfie.error_no_file: Please take or upload a selfie
upload_selfie.error_id_not_found: ID photo not found. Please go back and upload your ID first.

result.pending_msg: Your verification is not complete yet
result.pending_desc: You have completed ID verification. Please complete selfie verification to finish the process.
result.success_msg: Your identity has been successfully verified.
result.failed_msg: We could not verify your identity. Please review the issues below.
result.system_error_msg: A system error occurred during verification. Please try again.
result.image_lightbox_hint: Click outside or press Esc to close
result.no_data_msg: Verification details are not available yet.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.welcome_desc: Manage verifications, view statistics, and configure settings.
dashboard.webhooks_desc: Outbound delivery attempts to your webhook URL—configure the URL under API & webhooks in the nav.
dashboard.recent_webhook_failures_desc: Latest outbound deliveries that reported failure.
dashboard.no_webhook_failures: No failed webhook deliveries in the recent window.
dashboard.delete_verification_confirm: Erase this verification and related MongoDB records for your tenant? This cannot be undone. Set ADMIN_DELETE_PURGE_OBJECTS=1 on the API to best-effort delete linked R2 objects on erase.
dashboard.replay_webhook_tooltip: Rebuild terminal webhook from current profile state and queue delivery.
dashboard.replay_webhook_stored_tooltip: Use last saved webhook body from WebhookDelivery if available; otherwise same as Replay webhook.
dashboard.escalate_manual_review_confirm: Send this pending session to UNDER_REVIEW and notify your integration webhook (verification.manual_review_queued)?
dashboard.manual_review_desc: Sessions in UNDER_REVIEW. Approve or reject to emit a terminal verification webhook.
dashboard.manual_review_queued_ok: Session moved to UNDER_REVIEW; integration webhook delivery scheduled if configured.
dashboard.manual_review_resolve_ok: Decision saved; terminal verification webhook delivery scheduled.
dashboard.manual_review_assignee_prompt: Reviewer label or queue name (optional, max 160 chars). Leave blank if none:
dashboard.errors_insight_desc: Rough counts from recent ID and selfie validation rows (tenant-scoped).
dashboard.errors_insight_empty: No structured errors in the sampled window.
dashboard.capture_session_copied: Capture session header copied to clipboard.
dashboard.capture_session_copy_fallback: Copy this header value for mobile clients:
dashboard.capture_session_failed: Could not mint a capture session token. Is VERIFICATION_CAPTURE_TOKEN_SECRET set?
dashboard.webhook_replay_stored_payload: Terminal webhook replay scheduled using last stored delivery payload.
dashboard.webhook_replay_live_fallback: No stored payload for this terminal event; replay used fresh payload from profile.
dashboard.recent_profiles_desc: KYC profiles (one row per Mongo profile). Columns show checkpoint status derived from IdCardVerification and SelfieValidation.
dashboard.name: Name

settings.title: Settings
settings.reset_confirm: Are you sure you want to reset all settings to defaults?
settings.verification_rules_desc: Configure which verification steps are required for approval.
settings.require_id_card_desc: Users must upload and verify their ID card
settings.require_selfie_desc: Users must take and verify a selfie
settings.single_verification_warning: Only one verification method is enabled. This provides less security.
settings.id_thresholds_desc: Minimum confidence scores required for ID card verification.
settings.selfie_thresholds_desc: Minimum confidence scores required for selfie verification.
settings.age_constraints_desc: Minimum and maximum allowed ages for verification.
settings.api_keys_desc: Create and manage API keys for direct API access. Keys are shown only once.
settings.api_key_one_time: Copy this key now. You will not be able to see it again.
settings.api_key_revoke_confirm: Are you sure you want to revoke this API key?
settings.integration_moved_banner: Webhook URL, signing secrets, API keys, and IP allowlists are on the API & webhooks page—not here. This tab is only verification rules and thresholds.
settings.integration_webhook_desc: We POST JSON when you subscribe to an event. URL and secret are required for delivery. Signing secret is never shown again after save.
settings.integration_webhook_events_hint: Uncheck any type you don't want delivered. Uncheck all to disable automatic webhooks until you enable at least one type (replay from the dashboard still works).
settings.integration_webhook_events_hint_short: Choose which lifecycle transitions call your server. Uncheck all to pause automatic sends (dashboard replay still works).
settings.integration_webhook_data_fields_hint: When selected, these are merged into JSON `data` alongside `profileId`, `status`, etc. Email and full name are personally identifiable—enable only if your receiver needs them. Use `integrationExternalRef` and `integrationMetadata` on verify-id / verify-selfie to supply `externalRef` and `metadata`.
settings.integration_webhook_header_timestamp: X-Webhook-Timestamp: Unix seconds as a string
settings.integration_webhook_header_signature: X-Webhook-Signature: sha256=… HMAC of `{timestamp}.{exact raw body}`
settings.integration_webhook_delivery_id_body: The server appends a unique `id` field to the JSON body before computing the signature.
settings.integration_webhook_external_ref_hint: Persist `integrationExternalRef` (string) and/or `integrationMetadata` (string map) on ID or selfie verification requests so they can appear in webhooks when enabled above.
settings.integration_webhook_secret_placeholder: Leave blank to keep the current secret; enter a value to rotate
settings.integration_webhook_secret_missing: No signing secret configured—delivery will not fire until both URL and secret are set.
settings.integration_webhook_clear_confirm: Stop signing deliveries with the current secret? You can set a new one afterwards.
settings.integration_api_ips_desc: When you add entries, requests using X-API-Key must come from matching IPv4, IPv6, or CIDR ranges (similar to pinning access to trusted runners or Cloudflare tunnels). Clerk dashboard sessions are not restricted by this list.
settings.api_key_allowlist_label: Allowed IPs & CIDRs for this key
settings.api_key_allowlist_placeholder: One rule per line, or comma-separated. Example: 203.0.113.50 or 198.51.100.0/24 or 2001:db8::/32
settings.api_key_allowlist_hint_empty: Empty = allow any IP for this key.

profile.title: Profile

account.title: Account
account.subtitle: Your sign-in identity and plan usage. Connect backends via API & webhooks in the nav.
account.description: View your account identity used for dashboard access.
account.integrations_hint: Webhooks, API keys, and per-key IP allowlists live in one place.
account.name: Name

profile.usage_intro: Approximate verification counts for your current billing period (see billing docs for precise rules).

security.title: Security
security.description: Manage API keys used by your backend to call Armith APIs.
security.rotation_intro: Use overlap when changing secrets so integrations keep working.
security.rotation_keys: API keys: create a second key, deploy it, then revoke the old one. IP rules: account-wide list here; per-key lists on Growth+.
security.rotation_webhook: Webhook signing secret: briefly accept both secrets on your verifier, then rotate on the Webhooks tab (or PUT /admin/settings body.integration), then retire the old secret.
security.rotation_doc_hint: Webhooks tab for URL/signing secret; API keys tab for keys and IP allowlists—rotate with overlap.
security.tabs_hint: This tab handles API keys and IP rules. Dashboard Settings only adjusts verification—not webhooks.
security.webhook_moved_title: Outbound webhooks use the Webhooks tab
security.webhook_moved_desc: Configure HTTPS URL and signing secret there so it’s easy to find and share.
security.api_keys_where_allowlist: Set the account-wide IP list above for all keys. On paid plans you can also set per-key rules on each row.
security.allowlist_heading: Restrict this key by caller IP
security.allowlist_hint: One IPv4, IPv4 CIDR (e.g. 203.0.113.0/24), or exact IPv6 per line. Leave empty to allow all IPs. Requires correct proxy forwarded IP in production.
security.revoke_confirm: Are you sure you want to revoke this API key?
security.one_time: Copy this API key now. It will not be shown again.
